flood.description: Flood/heavy rainfall shock — city-specific based on geography and 2018-2020 flood data

Mangaluru.rationale: Coastal city, highest flood risk; Dakshina Kannada severely affected in 2019. Netravathi flooding disrupts water intake and food logistics.

Bengaluru.rationale: Urban flooding from lake breaches and stormwater; moderate impact on piped water (Cauvery source distant). Food supply chain partially disrupted by road damage.

Hubballi-Dharwad.rationale: Malaprabha basin flooding; interior location means road disruption isolates city. Moderate water and food impact.

Mysuru.rationale: KRS dam regulated; plateau city with minimal flood history. Low direct impact but some supply chain disruption from regional flooding.

Belagavi.rationale: Krishna sub-basin; surrounding taluks flood but city partially protected. Moderate supply chain disruption.

recovery_curve.description: Multiplier applied to shock magnitude at each month after shock onset. Gradual degradation then gradual recovery.
recovery_curve.interpretation: Month 0 of shock: supply = base × curve[0]. Each subsequent month moves to next value. 1.0 = full recovery.

flood.trigger_explanation: Karnataka SW Monsoon peak: Jul-Sep; 90%+ of floods occur during this window
flood.source: ActionplanforFloodriskmanagement2021.pdf, Table 2 (pp. 16-18), Chapter 1.8; city risk from Chapter 3 (Bengaluru UFM) and basin flood zonation

drought.description: Drought shock — city-specific based on hydrology and reservoir dependency

historical_context.drought_frequency: Karnataka has 10-80% increase in drought incidence projected under climate change
historical_context.affected_regions: North Karnataka (Belagavi, Hubballi-Dharwad) most vulnerable; semi-arid interior
historical_context.reservoir_dependency: Cauvery for Bengaluru/Mysuru, Malaprabha for Belagavi/Hubballi-Dharwad

Bengaluru.rationale: Cauvery-dependent; 2017 drought: boreholes dried, tanker dependency 3x. Large population amplifies per-capita shortage. High trust impact due to middle-class expectations.

Mysuru.rationale: Cauvery-dependent but smaller population. KRS dam provides some buffer. Moderate impact.

Mangaluru.rationale: Coastal high-rainfall region (~3500mm). Least drought-vulnerable among 5 cities. Groundwater recharge from Netravathi.

Hubballi-Dharwad.rationale: Semi-arid interior; Malaprabha reservoir critical and frequently low. North Karnataka drought belt. Highest water stress.

Belagavi.rationale: Rain-dependent zone; Malaprabha catchment. Frequent drought declarations in Belagavi district. Agricultural hinterland severely affected.

recovery_curve.description: Drought recovery is slower than flood (monsoon onset helps but reservoir refill takes months)
recovery_curve.interpretation: Drought recovery follows monsoon onset; interior cities recover slower than coastal.

drought.trigger_explanation: Pre-monsoon summer: Mar-May; reservoir levels at lowest, groundwater stress peak

economic_crisis.description: Economic shock — city-specific based on economic structure and vulnerability

Bengaluru.rationale: IT/services concentration makes Bengaluru most volatile. COVID-19 hit gig economy, startups, and informal IT workers hardest. Mass migrant exodus in 2020.

Mysuru.rationale: Tourism + heritage economy moderately affected. Manufacturing base provides some stability.

Mangaluru.rationale: Port + petrochemical economy provides essential services resilience. Trade disruption moderate.

Hubballi-Dharwad.rationale: Regional commerce hub; less globally connected = lower shock transmission. Agricultural trade continues.

Belagavi.rationale: Military base + sugar industry = stable employment floor. Least economic shock vulnerability.

recovery_curve.description: Economic recovery is slowest; follows GDP rebound trajectory
recovery_curve.interpretation: Income reduction applied at curve[0], then gradually recovers. Bengaluru slowest due to structural adjustment.

trust_mechanics.description: Formal definition of trust variable and its system effects

definition.meaning: Agent's confidence in government institutions, resource delivery reliability, and social safety nets. 1.0 = full trust, 0.0 = no trust.

resource_satisfaction.rule: if resource_received >= 0.8 * resource_demanded: trust += 0.02; else: trust -= 0.05 * (1 - received/demanded)
resource_satisfaction.explanation: Getting <80% of demanded resources erodes trust; adequate supply slowly builds trust

policy_benefit.rule: if agent receives Anna Bhagya/Gruha Jyothi/Gruha Lakshmi benefits: trust += 0.03

shock_event.explanation: Shocks directly reduce trust; magnitude varies by city and shock type

social_influence.explanation: Agent trust converges toward social network average (peer effects)

policy_effectiveness.explanation: Below 50% trust, policy schemes become less effective — agents distrust/don't access government programs

cooperation.explanation: Very low trust → agents stop cooperating, hoard resources, refuse collective action

protest_risk.explanation: Below 30% trust → agents may protest, further destabilizing the system (feedback loop)

demand_escalation.explanation: Low trust → agents hoard/stockpile → effective demand increases up to 20% (panic buying effect)

recovery.rule: Each month without shock: trust += 0.01 (capped at initial_value)
recovery.explanation: Trust slowly recovers in stable periods but takes many months to rebuild

trust_mechanics.source: ABM design parameter; thresholds informed by social trust literature (Ostrom 1990, Putnam 2000) and Karnataka post-disaster field reports

hces_context.description: HCES 2023-24 factsheet for poverty and consumption context
hces_context.note: Karnataka urban MPCE slightly below national average; informs demand calibration baseline

Bengaluru.flood_note: Urban flooding from lake breaches and stormwater overflow; Megha Sandesha flood model exists (Ch.3 Flood Report)
Bengaluru.drought_note: Cauvery-dependent; 2017 drought caused severe water tanker crisis
Bengaluru.economic_note: IT sector concentration = volatile to global economic shocks

Mysuru.flood_note: KRS dam regulated; city on plateau, minimal flood history
Mysuru.drought_note: Cauvery-dependent but smaller population = lower stress per capita

Mangaluru.flood_note: Coastal city; Netravathi flooding; 2019 floods severely affected Dakshina Kannada (Flood Report Table 2)
Mangaluru.drought_note: High rainfall region (~3500mm/year); coastal aquifers less drought-prone

Hubballi-Dharwad.flood_note: Interior city; Malaprabha basin flooding affects surrounding taluks
Hubballi-Dharwad.drought_note: Semi-arid interior; Malaprabha reservoir low; North Karnataka drought belt
Hubballi-Dharwad.economic_note: Regional commerce hub; less volatile than IT-driven cities

Belagavi.flood_note: Krishna sub-basin; some flooding in surrounding taluks but city less affected
Belagavi.drought_note: Rain-fed agriculture zone; Malaprabha catchment; frequent drought declarations
Belagavi.economic_note: Military + sugar industry; relatively stable base
